text.title: Modern BA Framework: BABOK for Agile, AI/ML, Cloud, and Platform Delivery Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the modern-ba-framework methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale and is referenced from `content/06-decision-tree.xml`.

rule.statement: Step 1 (declare perspectives): List explicit perspective set; reject 'agile' as the default-without-justification.
rule.rationale: Skipping or reordering this step breaks the methodology's invariants; it is the declare perspectives gate.

rule.statement: Step 2 (adapt kas): For each KA, list perspective-specific tasks/deliverables.
rule.rationale: Skipping or reordering this step breaks the methodology's invariants; it is the adapt kas gate.

rule.statement: Step 3 (template deliverables): Pick correct template variants per perspective.
rule.rationale: Skipping or reordering this step breaks the methodology's invariants; it is the template deliverables gate.

rule.statement: Step 4 (review): Cross-functional review; verify each perspective has concrete deliverables.
rule.rationale: Skipping or reordering this step breaks the methodology's invariants; it is the review gate.

rule.statement: If preconditions for modern-ba-framework do not hold, do not apply this methodology; route to an alternative as the decision tree suggests.
rule.rationale: Methodologies misapplied to wrong contexts create overhead without value; explicit skip preserves the agent's budget.
